page: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a virtual private server belonging to the High Performance family. It features 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 12.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of a 260 GB NVMe SSD. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results indicate a mix of performance tiers. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, ranking in the top 25 percent with a score of 2427.57. However, multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category with a stress-ng score of 9526.81. Memory performance shows significant variance; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 380.64 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weak, falling into the bottom 25 percent. Passmark memory and database operations scores are poor, placing in the bottom 10 percent.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is reflected in the lower multi-threaded decompression and database benchmark scores. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high capacity of NVMe SSD storage relative to its shared CPU class, making it suitable for workloads that require fast local storage but do not demand sustained, non-shared CPU performance. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, light text generation with small language models, and general-purpose applications with low-concurrency database requirements.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 12.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 260 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark data reveals a strong single-core CPU performance rating, though multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into average and weak categories, respectively. Specifically, large-block uncached memory read and write speeds are weak, and Passmark database operations rank in the bottom 10 percent. Given the shared CPU allocation and the balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated compute resources. It is suited for static web serving, basic application hosting, and small-scale text generation tasks.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a virtual server configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 12.0 GB of RAM, and 260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 12.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 260 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining strong single-core execution with high-capacity local NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
12.0 GB of system memory and 260 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Weak large-block uncached memory bandwidth and database operations
Best suited for static web serving and light general-purpose applications
